info.name: NBES — RBAC + Sprint 1.3 (Audit / SecOps / Dashboards)
info.description:
End-to-end test suite for the NBES backend.

## Scope

- **Sprint 1.2** — Roles, Permissions & RBAC Gateway (folders 1–4)
- **Sprint 1.3** — Audit hardening, Security Operations Console, Role Dashboard Skeletons, System 17 idempotency (folders 5–8)

## Two ways to get tokens

### A. Through IAM + Keycloak (recommended — exercises the production token path)

1. Boot IAM:
   ```bash
   cd iam && docker compose up -d keycloak postgres
   python manage.py migrate
   python manage.py seed_superadmins
   python manage.py seed_systems
   python manage.py provision_keycloak_clients
   python manage.py seed_system_roles
   ```
2. Boot NBES with `KEYCLOAK_ENABLED=True` pointing at the local Keycloak:
   ```bash
   cd ../nbes-backend
   docker compose up -d db redis web
   docker compose exec web python manage.py migrate
   ```
3. Run **folder `0 · Setup`** in this collection. The first request (`Keycloak token (super_admin)`) does a Keycloak resource-owner-password-grant against the `clet-iam-internal` client and stashes the access token into the `admin_token` variable. NBES validates the RS256 signature via the JWKS path.
4. To test narrower roles (auditor, security_officer, examiner) you need users with those role assignments. Invite via IAM (`POST /api/v1/admin/invite/` or the UI), then run the corresponding `Keycloak token (...)` request with their credentials.

### B. HS256 dev mint (no IAM needed, no Keycloak)

Set `KEYCLOAK_ENABLED=False` in `nbes-backend/.env`. Mint tokens with the snippet at the bottom of this description, paste into the variable boxes. NBES skips the JWKS path.

```python
# mint_token.py — run as: python mint_token.py auditor
import jwt, uuid, time, sys
ROLE = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 'system_administrator'
SECRET = 'shared-jwt-secret-between-iam-and-nbes'   # JWT_SECRET_KEY in .env
payload = {
    'sub':   str(uuid.uuid4()),
    'email': f'{ROLE}@example.com',
    'iat':   int(time.time()),
    'exp':   int(time.time()) + 3600,
    'realm_access':    {'roles': ['super_admin'] if ROLE == 'super_admin' else []},
    'resource_access': {'nbes-api': {'roles': [ROLE]}},
}
print(jwt.encode(payload, SECRET, algorithm='HS256'))
```

## Notes

- Every mutation writes an `AuditEvent`. Every 403 writes `AUTHZ_DENIED` + a `SecurityEvent(authz_denied)`.
- State-mutating verbs (`POST`/`PUT`/`PATCH`/`DELETE`) require an `Idempotency-Key` header — the `IdempotencyKeyMiddleware` rejects them otherwise (folder 8 covers this).
- The edge throttle activates at 100 rejected (401/403/429) responses from one IP in 15 minutes (folder 8).

item.name: 0 · Setup — Get tokens (Keycloak direct)
item.description:
Resource-owner-password-grant against `clet-iam-internal`. Returns a real RS256 token NBES validates via JWKS. Run the user you care about; subsequent requests pick up the saved token.

If the user does not exist yet, invite them through IAM first.

request.description: Resource-owner-password-grant. Bypasses IAM's MFA flow (intentional — MFA is fiddly to automate). The token still has the correct realm/client roles attached by the audience-resolve mapper.

exec: // Makes no HTTP call. Decodes the cached admin_token and asserts the shape.

request.description: Calls a benign endpoint just so the request runs; the meaningful work is in the test script which decodes the admin_token and confirms the claim shape.

Returns what NBES thinks the bearer can do. `roles_in_jwt` is straight from the token; `roles_recognised_by_nbes` is the subset that maps to an active `Role` row; `permissions` is the resolved set the gateway enforces.

Use this to confirm the admin token works before running any mutation.
Lists every codename NBES enforces. Seeded from `apps/users/migrations/0001_initial.py` plus 0005 (audit:search/verify), 0006 (secops:view), 0007 (dashboards:manage).
Lists every role NBES recognises. Sprint 1.3 adds `security_officer`.

Tells NBES it should recognise a role name. Idempotent: re-posting the same name returns the existing row with 200 OK.

Note the `Idempotency-Key` header — mandatory on all state-mutating verbs.
Marks the role inactive. Historical audit rows still resolve. Users holding this role lose every NBES permission within 60 s.

item.name: 4 · Denial scenarios
item.description: Confirms the gateway rejects unauthorised callers AND records both an audit event AND a SecurityEvent(authz_denied).

request.description: An item_writer should never see the admin surface. Expect 403 AUTHZ_DENIED, an `AuditEvent(action="AUTHZ_DENIED")` row, and a `SecurityEvent(category="authz_denied")` row (new in Sprint 1.3).

item.name: 5 · Audit (Sprint 1.3)
item.description: The Auditor's three surfaces — search, hash-chain proof, NDJSON export. Plus a forced denial via the writer token to prove the new audit:* codenames gate as expected.

request.description: GET /api/v1/audit/search — paginated, filterable. Every call writes its own `AUDIT_SEARCH` AuditEvent (meta-audit).

Hash-chain proof for one UTC day. Returns the head hash + (once notarised) the System 22 anchor reference.

First run the daily Celery task manually so an anchor exists:
```bash
docker compose exec web python manage.py shell -c 'from apps.audit.tasks import daily_hash_anchor; print(daily_hash_anchor.run())'
```

Then set the `yesterday_utc` variable to e.g. `2026-05-19`.

response.name: 404 — no anchor yet

request.description: Streamed NDJSON, one event per line. Suitable for judicial-review bundles. Use `--output -O` with curl to capture the suggested filename from `Content-Disposition`.
item_writer holds neither `audit:search` nor wildcard; expect 403 plus AuditEvent(AUTHZ_DENIED) + SecurityEvent(authz_denied).

item.name: 6 · Security Operations (Sprint 1.3)
item.description: The Security Officer's live view. Every endpoint is gated by `secops:view`, granted to `security_officer` and `system_administrator`.

request.description: Joins SecurityEvent rows from the last 24h with the live Redis sentinels tracking active throttles/blocks. `retry_after_seconds` is the TTL remaining on the sentinel.

Returns SecurityEvent rows with category `anomaly_detected`. The detector itself is COULD per blueprint §1.9 and not in Sprint 1.3 — this endpoint exists so the dashboard contract is stable. Returns `[]`.
Per-day rollup. Mirrors the payload the `daily_security_summary` Celery task emails the Security Officer at 06:00 UTC. Omit `date` to default to yesterday UTC.

item.name: 7 · Dashboards (Sprint 1.3)
item.description: Role-specific dashboard skeletons. Frontend reads `/dashboard/me` on login and renders the panels for the user's roles.

exec: // Mint a fresh idempotency key and stash it so the replay test below uses the same one.

request.description:
Admin-only (`dashboards:manage`). PATCH the panel's display_order, name, is_active, or default_config — no redeploy needed.

`idempotency_key` is set by the pre-request script so the **Replay** request below uses the same key.
Same key as the previous request → IdempotencyKeyMiddleware returns the cached response and the `X-Idempotent-Replay: true` response header.
Idempotency-Key is required on every state-mutating verb. Expect `IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REQUIRED` / 400.

response.name: 400 IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REQUIRED

item.name: 9 · User Administration
item.description: User profile CRUD, bulk import, and bulk role assignment. All endpoints require users:manage or users:import permission.

request.description: Paginated list of user profiles. Optional ?status=, ?role=, ?search= filters.
Provision a user in Keycloak and create a local profile. Saves user_id for downstream requests.
200 = direct assign; 202 = high-privilege role queued for two-admin approval; 409 = mutual-exclusion conflict or already assigned.

formdata.description: CSV or XLSX file with columns: first_name, last_name, email, roles (comma-separated). Optional: national_id, department.

request.description: Partial-success: valid rows are provisioned; invalid rows are reported in row_errors. Requires users:import permission (step-up auth). Supported types: text/csv, .xlsx.

Assign or revoke a single role across up to 500 existing profiles. High-privilege roles (director_general, system_administrator, nbec_member) are blocked — use the individual /roles/ endpoint instead. Mutual-exclusion and already-assigned checks are applied per user; failures are reported row-level without aborting the batch. Requires users:manage + step-up auth.

item.name: 8 · Edge throttle smoke (manual)
item.description:
Fire 100+ requests with a deliberately bad token. The 101st should be 429 with `Retry-After: 900`. Run from a shell rather than Postman — Postman doesn't loop tidily, and the throttle keys on IP so a single request body won't move the counter past the threshold quickly.

```bash
for i in $(seq 1 105); do
  curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" \
    http://localhost:8003/api/v1/audit/search \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer not-a-real-token"
done | sort | uniq -c
# Expect: ~100 × 401, 5 × 429
```

Then run **6 · Currently throttled / blocked IPs** above to see the throttled IP listed with `active=true` and a `retry_after_seconds` close to 900.

request.description: Single firing of the request that the shell loop above will fire 105 times. Useful for inspecting the response shape once.

response.name: 429 Throttled (101st onward, with Retry-After)
